baseline.evidence: Skill-less haiku run 2026-06-12, scratch _scratch/red-baseline-qa/. The unaided agent DID run a real Lighthouse audit via the chrome-devtools MCP, but the QA it produced is not gate-able and is partly wrong: (QA-F1) the lighthouse_audit tool it used 'excludes performance', so the Performance score is 'Not measured' — yet Performance >=95 is a hard requirement; it issued a go/no-go verdict on a QA run that never measured Performance. (QA-F2) Core Web Vitals (LCP/CLS/INP) were 'Not captured by testing environment' — the green-CWV gate was never actually run. (QA-F3) the deliverable is a prose QA_REPORT.md (human markdown), not a machine-readable structured report a gate could verify. (QA-F4) the schema section lists 'Missing: Organization schema, SoftwareApplication schema, FAQPage/HowTo' — recommending FAQPage and HowTo, which are dead/banned rich-result types (FAQ rich results ended May 2026, HowTo Sept 2023); the agent's schema knowledge is stale. (QA-F5) it returned NO-GO partly because it COULD NOT measure Performance/CWV, conflating 'measured-and-failed' with 'unable-to-measure' — a partial QA run was treated as a usable gate result. Measured facts it did get: accessibility 91 (< 95), best-practices 100, SEO 100, zero console errors, zero structured data present.

engine_selftest.evidence:
$ python3 scripts/qa_gate.py --selftest
SELFTEST RESULT: PASS (1 good, 12 bad, 1 invariant)
exit 0

Bad fixture coverage (12 bad cases): q1-performance-absent refused (performance key missing from lighthouse -> Q1); q1-empty-pages refused (pages=[] -> Q1); q1-cwv-missing refused (cwv block absent -> Q1); q1-console-errors-missing refused (console_errors absent -> Q1); q2-accessibility-below-threshold refused (accessibility=91 < 95 -> Q2); q3-lcp-over-threshold refused (lcp_ms=3000 > 2500 -> Q3); q3-inp-absent refused (inp_ms missing -> Q3); q4-console-errors-present refused (1 console error -> Q4); q5-faqpage-banned refused (FAQPage in types -> Q5); q5-howto-banned refused (HowTo in types -> Q5); q6-tool-missing refused (tool field absent -> Q6); q6-date-missing refused (date field absent -> Q6). Invariant: empty pages list flips PASS to FAIL.

skill_md.evidence: Filled skill-md-template.md: name=qa-gate-runner matches directory; description starts 'Use when' with triggers 'run the QA gates', 'Lighthouse check before launch', 'is this ready to ship', 'check Core Web Vitals / console errors / schema'; ends with 'Do not use for writing app code, subjective design review, or non-web artifacts'; third person, no workflow summary. IRON LAWS: 6 laws contiguous 1-6, all traced to law_origins. Overview quotes verbatim baseline failures: 'Not measured', 'Not captured by testing environment', 'FAQPage and HowTo', 'accessibility 91'. Rationalization table: 6 rows quoting baseline excuses. Red flags: 6 observable states with stage pointers.

evals.evidence: evals/evals.json: skill='qa-gate-runner' matches dir; notes >= 200 chars with 'baseline' present (notes = 768 chars); 6 evals, one per IRON LAW; laws 1-6 each cited in asserts: Law 1 in eval[0] (IRON LAW 1), Law 2 in eval[1] (IRON LAW 2), Law 3 in eval[2] (IRON LAW 3), Law 4 in eval[3] (IRON LAW 4), Law 5 in eval[4] (IRON LAW 5), Law 6 in eval[5] (IRON LAW 6); triggers block has 8 should_trigger and 8 should_not_trigger; all eval prompts >= 20 chars; all evals have >= 3 asserts.

self_gate.evidence:
$ python3 ../skill-forge/scripts/skill_gate.py qa-gate-runner  (from skills/)
SKILL GATE: PASS (6 laws, package + ledger verified)
exit 0

green_check.evidence:
Wrote a complete all-green two-page qa-report.json at _scratch/green-verify-qa/qa-report.json (tool='lighthouse', date='2026-06-12T11:30:00Z'; page 1: staging.timecardpro.io/ performance=97/accessibility=98/best_practices=100/seo=100, cwv lcp_ms=1100/cls=0.01/inp_ms=90, console_errors=[], schema types=[SoftwareApplication] valid=true; page 2: /calculator performance=96/accessibility=99/best_practices=100/seo=98, cwv lcp_ms=950/cls=0.0/inp_ms=110, console_errors=[], schema types=[SoftwareApplication,BreadcrumbList] valid=true) and ran:
$ python3 scripts/qa_gate.py _scratch/green-verify-qa/qa-report.json
QA GATE RESULT: PASS
exit 0

Per-law compliance: Q1 (Law 1): all 4 lighthouse scores present in 0-100 for both pages; cwv, console_errors, schema all present — PASS. Baseline QA-F1 (Performance excluded) does NOT occur. Q2 (Law 5): all scores >=95 for both pages — PASS. Baseline QA-F5 (accessibility=91) does NOT occur. Q3 (Law 2): lcp_ms=1100<=2500, cls=0.01<=0.1, inp_ms=90<=200 for page 1; lcp_ms=950, cls=0.0, inp_ms=110 for page 2 — PASS. Baseline QA-F2 (CWV not captured) does NOT occur. Q4: console_errors=[] for both pages — PASS. Q5 (Law 4): types only SoftwareApplication/BreadcrumbList, no FAQPage/HowTo, valid=true — PASS. Baseline QA-F4 (FAQPage/HowTo recommended) does NOT occur. Q6 (Law 3): tool='lighthouse' and date present; both pages have url — PASS. Baseline QA-F3 (prose QA_REPORT.md) does NOT occur.

law_origins.note: baseline QA-F1/F5: agent issued a go/no-go verdict with Performance 'Not measured' — a partial QA run was treated as a usable gate result; the law requires all 4 categories present or the report is not a QA run
baseline QA-F2: CWV (LCP/CLS/INP) were 'Not captured by testing environment' — the green-CWV gate was never run; the law requires numeric CWV metrics in the green range
baseline QA-F3: deliverable was a prose QA_REPORT.md (human markdown) not a machine-readable JSON a gate could verify; the law requires a structured qa-report.json

baseline QA-F4: schema section recommended FAQPage and HowTo — both dead/banned rich-result types (FAQ ended May 2026, HowTo Sept 2023); the law bans those types and restricts to {SoftwareApplication, BreadcrumbList}
baseline QA-F5: accessibility scored 91 (< 95 threshold) and was captured as a partial verdict rather than a hard block; the law sets >= 95 as the floor for every category

house-standard fail-closed engine with selftest: the gate is non-negotiable; a verdict issued without running qa_gate.py and pasting literal output is a violation; this law also counters the baseline's visual-review-only approach

findings: scripts/qa_gate.py:69: 🟡 PATCH: Q1 enforces completeness WITHIN each reported page (all 4 lighthouse scores, cwv, console_errors, schema) but cannot verify the report covers EVERY built page — an agent could report only the passing index page and omit a failing tool page, and the gate would PASS (pages non-empty, Q6 needs only one url). Fix: have qa_gate.py optionally accept the site dir or sitemap.xml and assert one report entry per built HTML page, or require the SKILL body to derive the page list from the sitemap. Not applied — the proper fix is a design expansion best done in the forge build.

scripts/qa_gate.py:210: 🔵 NIT: the whole report can be passed by fabricated all-green numbers — the gate validates structure + thresholds but cannot confirm the values came from a real Lighthouse/CWV run (same inherent ceiling as legal-crawl L7, ship S1, seo-content C6). Q6's tool+date guard only stops the laziest stubs; the skill body's 'run a real pass' instruction plus reviewer/human spot-check are the real mitigation.
scripts/qa_gate.py:218: 🔵 NIT: Q6 accepts any non-empty string for date, so a stub with "date": "done" passes. Tighten to require an ISO-8601-ish value (at least a 4-digit year) to reject lazy stubs.

scripts/qa_gate.py:36: 🔵 NIT: Q5 ALLOWED_SCHEMA_TYPES is a fixed {SoftwareApplication, BreadcrumbList}; a future tool legitimately needing e.g. WebApplication would false-FAIL (fail-closed-safe). Revisit the set if the portfolio adds a tool type that needs another schema.

$ python3 skill-forge/scripts/skill_gate.py qa-gate-runner
SKILL GATE: PASS (6 laws, package + ledger verified)
exit 0

SELFTEST RESULT: PASS (1 good, 12 bad, 1 invariant)
exit 0

adversarial.loophole_tried: report only the passing index page and omit a failing tool page — pages is non-empty and Q6 needs only one url, so the partial-coverage report passes although the real site has a failing page
report lab CWV that pass thresholds while real-user field CWV would be red, or fabricate green numbers — Q3 catches absent/breaching metrics; fabrication is the universal artifact-trust limit
hand the gate a prose QA_REPORT.md instead of JSON
include FAQPage or HowTo in schema.types, or set valid:true on actually-invalid schema
report a fabricated 97 for a category that really scored 91
issue a go/no-go verdict from visual review without running qa_gate.py or pasting its output
